affected_pattern: Enterprise latency-sensitive customer breaches latency commitment for 12 consecutive days
root_cause: Latency SLA measurement window drifted — the probe schedule was coarsened from 1-minute to 5-minute samples during a cost-cutting change, causing short latency spikes to be smoothed out of reporting but still breach the contract when measured externally.
supporting_evidence: SLA Breach RCA Playbook: Observability Granularity Drift pattern
Change ticket CHG-55102 coarsened probe schedule
Customer-side synthetic checks report latency breaches not seen internally
recommended_action: Restore 1-minute probe granularity on committed-latency circuits
Issue latency-breach credits per the contract
Align internal and customer-facing SLA measurement methodology
Document SLA measurement methodology in the contract appendix
